blocks.text: This letter sets out the terms on which Pinebrook Advisory Group LLC will conduct an operational review for {{fact:f:E-2019-001.client}}, and it is meant to be read in full before it is signed. I keep a simple rule for anything that leaves this office: a client should be able to see the whole of an engagement on a single page and know exactly what we will do, who will do it, what it costs, and when it starts. What follows is that page.

The review covers your core operating functions: how work moves through the business, where time and cost accumulate, and which of those points will repay attention first. We spend our hours where the work actually happens rather than in a conference room. That means observing your operations on site, reading the records you already keep, and sitting with the people who run the day to day. You will not be handed a survey of everything that might be improved. You will be handed a short, ordered list of the things worth doing something about, each one concrete enough to act on.

Two of our consultants will carry the work. Jennifer Fletcher, our Senior Consultant, will lead the engagement and be your point of contact for how it is run; she holds the schedule and makes certain the numbers reconcile before anything is put in front of you. Robert Miller, our Principal Consultant, will spend the days on your floor, and the recommendations you read at the close will be grounded in what he sees there. I read everything before it reaches you and stand behind all of it.

Our fee for the review is a fixed fee of {{fact:f:E-2019-001.fee}}, which covers the work described above from start through delivery. There are no hourly charges to track and no reconciliation at the end. Any out-of-pocket costs, should they arise, would be modest and billed only as incurred, with documentation. We will begin the work on {{fact:f:E-2019-001.start}} and expect the review to run about three months from that point.

If this reflects your understanding, please sign below and return one copy to my attention. If anything here reads differently than you expected, call me before you sign; a scope is far easier to settle now than in the middle of the work. We are glad to have the engagement and mean to give you reason to call us again.
